requirement: AI chatbot using Gemini that acts as GarudaX Chief Architect for MSE pitch sessions

tasks.title: Knowledge base for system prompt
tasks.description:
Create the comprehensive knowledge base files that will be concatenated into the Gemini system prompt.

Create directory: src/architect-bot/knowledge/

File 1: src/architect-bot/knowledge/garudax-overview.md
- GarudaX is a multi-tenant, AI-native securities exchange platform
- Architecture: 11 Go microservices + 3 React SPAs + FIX gateway
- Services: matching-engine, clearing-engine, margin-engine, settlement-engine, auth-service, compliance-service, market-data-service, warehouse-service, gateway, securities-service, platform-service
- Multi-tenant: X-GarudaX-Tenant header, tenant middleware, platform control plane
- Current tenants: mse-equities (Mongolian Stock Exchange, ACTIVE, flagship), ace-commodities (suspended)
- Tech stack: Go 1.25, React 19, PostgreSQL (TimescaleDB), Kafka, Redis, Docker, Kubernetes
- 65 domain types, 48 store interfaces, 94 handler files, 2540+ tests
- Securities module: instruments (equity+bond), order matching (price-time priority), T+2 settlement, corporate actions, surveillance (12 alert patterns), day lifecycle state machine
- FIX 4.4 protocol gateway with TCP session management, 95.3% parser coverage
- Native binary protocol codec (92.5% coverage)

File 2: src/architect-bot/knowledge/millenniumit-comparison.md
- Copy key sections from docs/millenniumit-gap-analysis.md
- 86 features scored: FULL 3, SUBSTANTIAL 28, BASIC 35, STUB 8, MISSING 12
- GarudaX at 47% depth vs MillenniumIT
- Key GarudaX advantages over MIT: multi-tenant (MIT is single-venue), AI-native, modern stack, FIX 4.4, comprehensive test suite, open infrastructure
- What MIT has that GarudaX doesn't: 20+ years production hardening, partitions, definition/template system, rule builder

File 3: src/architect-bot/knowledge/securities-domain.md
- Order types: LIMIT, MARKET, STOP-LIMIT, STOP-MARKET, ICEBERG
- Time-in-force: DAY, GTC, GTD, IOC, FOK
- Matching: price-time priority with iceberg support, self-trade prevention
- Auction: call auction with price-ladder clearing price algorithm, random end
- Day lifecycle: CLOSED → PRE_OPEN → TRADING (continuous) → POST_CLOSE → CLOSED
- Settlement: T+2, DVP (delivery vs payment), settlement obligations, netting
- Corporate actions: dividends, stock splits, rights issues, entitlement processing
- Surveillance: 12 alert patterns (large trade, price spike, wash trade, spoofing, layering, etc.)
- Circuit breakers: static + dynamic price bands, configurable per instrument
- Trading parameters: tick tables, lot sizes, allowed order types per instrument
- Indices: weighted basket calculation, real-time recalculation
- Instrument folders: hierarchical grouping
- Entity permissions: role-based CRUD matrix

File 4: src/architect-bot/knowledge/mse-context.md
- Mongolia Stock Exchange (MSE): established 1991, ~200 listed companies
- Current system: MillenniumIT (London Stock Exchange Technology)
- MSE challenges: aging technology, high licensing costs, limited customization
- FRC (Financial Regulatory Commission): regulatory body, daily reporting requirements
- MCSD (Mongolian Central Securities Depository): settlement and custody
- GarudaX value proposition for MSE: modern platform, lower TCO, AI-native operations, multi-tenant for future venue expansion
- Settlement: MNT (Mongolian Tugrik), T+2 cycle, MCSD integration planned

File 5: src/architect-bot/knowledge/competitive-landscape.md
- Nasdaq Market Technology: SMARTS surveillance, Genium INET matching
- NYSE/ICE: Pillar trading platform
- London Stock Exchange: MillenniumIT (Sri Lanka), Turquoise MTF
- HKEX: Orion Trading Platform (OTP)
- Deutsche Börse: T7 trading system
- Other vendors: Aquis Technologies, TradingScreen, Exactpro
- GarudaX differentiation: multi-tenant (one platform, many venues), AI-native, open infrastructure, modern Go+React stack

Keep each file concise but authoritative. Total combined size should be under 15KB to fit Gemini context.

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md

tasks.title: Architect-bot backend service
tasks.description:
Create the Express.js backend service that proxies chat to Gemini.

Directory: src/architect-bot/

Files to create:

1. src/architect-bot/package.json
   - name: @garudax/architect-bot
   - dependencies: express, @google/generative-ai, cors
   - scripts: start, dev

2. src/architect-bot/server.js
   - Express server on port 3010
   - CORS enabled (allow demo.garudax.asla.mn, admin.garudax.asla.mn)
   - POST /api/chat — main endpoint
     - Request: { message: string, history?: { role: string, parts: string }[] }
     - Response: { response: string } (streamed if possible, otherwise full response)
   - GET /api/health — health check
   - On startup, load all knowledge/*.md files, concatenate into system prompt
   - System prompt format:
     ```
     You are the Chief Architect of GarudaX, a multi-tenant AI-native securities exchange platform.
     You are presenting GarudaX to the Mongolian Stock Exchange (MSE) board and technical team.
     
     Your persona:
     - Confident but honest — acknowledge gaps when asked directly
     - Technical depth — you know the codebase intimately (65 types, 48 stores, 94 handlers)
     - Strategic vision — you can explain WHY GarudaX is better than MillenniumIT for MSE
     - Business-aware — you understand TCO, time-to-market, and operational efficiency
     
     When answering:
     - Be concise but thorough
     - Use specific numbers and technical details
     - Compare to MillenniumIT when relevant
     - Speak about GarudaX capabilities with confidence
     - If asked about something GarudaX doesn't have, be honest but pivot to strengths
     
     KNOWLEDGE BASE:
     {concatenated knowledge files}
     ```
   - Gemini API key from env: GEMINI_API_KEY
   - Model: gemini-2.0-flash
   - Max tokens: 1024
   - Temperature: 0.7

3. src/architect-bot/Dockerfile
   - FROM node:22-alpine
   - WORKDIR /app
   - COPY package*.json → npm install --production
   - COPY . .
   - EXPOSE 3010
   - CMD ["node", "server.js"]

4. Add to docker-compose.yml:
   ```yaml
   architect-bot:
     build: src/architect-bot
     ports:
       - '3010:3010'
     environment:
       - GEMINI_API_KEY=AIzaSyBTkTNskrYdKBT_bw9ihEWQHdbs3yUS290
       - PORT=3010
     restart: unless-stopped
   ```

5. Add nginx route in /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/garudax.asla.mn:
   - Under the demo.garudax.asla.mn server block, add:
     ```
     location /api/architect/ {
         proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3010/api/;
         proxy_set_header Host $host;
         proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
         add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' $cors_origin always;
         add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' 'Content-Type' always;
         add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' 'POST, GET, OPTIONS' always;
     }
     ```

Test: npm start, then curl -X POST http://localhost:3010/api/chat -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message":"What is GarudaX?"}'

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T2.md

tasks.title: Chat widget in demo-runner
tasks.description:
Add an 'Ask the Architect' chat widget to the demo-runner SPA.

Files to create/modify:

1. src/demo-runner/src/components/ArchitectChat.tsx
   - Floating button (bottom-right, fixed position): brain/chat icon + 'Ask the Architect'
   - On click, expands to chat panel (400px wide, 500px tall)
   - Chat panel has:
     - Header: 'GarudaX Architect' with close button
     - Message list: user messages (right-aligned, blue) and bot messages (left-aligned, dark)
     - Input field + send button at bottom
   - Bot responses rendered as markdown (use a simple markdown-to-html function, no heavy lib)
   - Loading indicator while waiting for response
   - Conversation persisted in component state (lost on page refresh is OK)
   - API call: POST to /api/architect/chat with { message, history }
   - The gateway URL from DemoContext should be used, but for architect bot the URL is relative: /api/architect/chat (proxied by nginx)

2. src/demo-runner/src/components/ArchitectChat.module.css
   - Dark theme matching demo-runner styling
   - Floating button: round, 56px, gradient background, box-shadow
   - Chat panel: dark background, rounded corners, slide-up animation
   - Messages: bubbles with proper spacing
   - Input: dark input with send button
   - Responsive: on mobile, chat panel goes full-width

3. src/demo-runner/src/App.tsx
   - Import and render <ArchitectChat /> at the top level (outside section content)

Do NOT use any new npm dependencies. Use fetch API for HTTP calls. Simple markdown rendering (bold, italic, lists, code blocks) using string replace.

Build: cd src/demo-runner && npm run build

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T3.md

tasks.title: Deploy and test end-to-end
tasks.description:
Deploy architect-bot, rebuild demo-runner, configure nginx, and test.

1. cd src/architect-bot && npm install
2. docker compose build architect-bot
3. docker compose up -d architect-bot
4. Verify: curl http://localhost:3010/api/health
5. Update nginx config for demo.garudax.asla.mn to proxy /api/architect/
6. sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
7. docker compose build demo-runner && docker compose up -d demo-runner
8. Test through nginx: curl -X POST https://demo.garudax.asla.mn/api/architect/chat -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message":"What is GarudaX?"}'
9. Verify the chat widget appears on demo.garudax.asla.mn

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T4.md
